(a) Ammonia $\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)$
(b) Water $\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)$ act as reducing agents
(c) Carbon monoxide (CO)
(d) Hydrogen
Hint-Reducing agents are those substances which have the ability of adding hydrogen or removing oxygen from the other substances.

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — HOTS Questions — Q4
examples.problem:
Write balanced equations for the reactions between each of the following oxides and water :
(a) $\mathrm{Li}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) CaO
(c) $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$
examples.solution:
(a) $\mathrm{Li}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{LiOH}$
(b) $\mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{SO}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — HOTS Questions — Q5
examples.problem: Represent the following chemical reaction as a balanced equation. Liquid "rubbing alcohol," $\left(\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}\right)$ reacts with oxygen to form carbon dioxide gas and water.
examples.solution:
First, represent the name of the species involved as reactants and products with formulae and indicate their physical states.
$$
\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})
$$
Next, balance carbon. Place a coefficient of 3 in front of $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$ to balance the carbons in $\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}$. Balance the eight hydrogens in $\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}$ by adding a coefficient of 4 in front of $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$.
$\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
Now note that there are 10 oxygens on the right. On the left, one oxygen is in $\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}$ so nine are needed from $\mathrm{O}_{2}$. To get an odd number of oxygens from $\mathrm{O}_{2}$ we need to use a fractional coefficient, in this case $\frac{9}{2}$ (i.e., $\frac{9}{2} \times 2=9$ )
$$
\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\frac{9}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})
$$
Finally, we need to clear the fraction so that all coefficients are whole numbers. Multiply the whole equation by 2 and do a quick check.
$$
2 \mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+9 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 6 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+8 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})
$$

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — HOTS Questions — Q6
examples.problem:
(a) Would you expect aluminum oxide to be a solid, liquid, or gas at room temperature?
(b) Write the balanced chemical equation for the reaction of aluminum oxide with nitric acid.
examples.solution:
(a) Because aluminum oxide is the oxide of a metal, we would expect it to be an ionic solid. Indeed it is, and it has a very high melting point, $2072^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.
(b) In its compounds, aluminum has a $3+$ charge, $\mathrm{Al}^{3+}$, the oxide ion is $\mathrm{O}^{2-}$, consequently, the formula of aluminum oxide is $\mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$. Metal oxides tend to be basic and therefore reacts with acids to form salts and water. In this case the salt formed is aluminum nitrate, $\mathrm{Al}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{3}$. The balanced chemical equation is
$\mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+6 \mathrm{HNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Al}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — HOTS Questions — Q7
examples.problem:
Consider the following two possible reactions. If a reaction does occur, write the balanced equations illustrating the reactions.
(a) A strip of tin metal is placed in an aqueous $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$ solution.
(b) A strip of silver metal is placed in an aqueous perchloric acid solution.
examples.solution:
In the activity series that Sn is higher in the series than Ag . Therefore Sn replaces $\mathrm{Ag}+$ ions from solution.
(a) $\mathrm{Sn}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Sn}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})$
(b) In the activity series, $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ is in higher position than Ag . Therefore, $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ replaces $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$, but the reverse reaction, the replacement of $\mathrm{H}^{+}$by Ag , does not occur. No reaction occurs.

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — HOTS Questions — Q8
examples.problem:
Write combination reactions that occur when the metal barium reacts with the following nonmetals.
(a) Hydrogen
(b) Sulfur
(c) Bromine
(d) Nitrogen
examples.solution:
(a) $\mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaH}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})$
(b) $8 \mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{S}_{8}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 8 \mathrm{BaS}(\mathrm{s})$
(c) $\mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Br}_{2}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaBr}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})$
(d) $3 \mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{N}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ba}_{3} \mathrm{~N}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})$

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — Case Study Based MCQs — Q1
examples.problem:
Read the following and answer any four questions from (i) to (v).
Chemical reactions involve the breaking and making of bonds between atoms to produce new substances. during a chemical reaction atoms of one element do not change into those of another element. Nor do atoms disappear from the mixture or appear from elsewhere. There are certain types of reactions. Reactions in which a single product is formed from two or more reactants is known as a combination reaction. Decomposition reactions are the reactions in which a compound breaks down into simpler compounds. Displacement and double displacement reactions are the one in which an atom or group of atom is replaced by another.Another type of reaction is redox reactions in which simultaneous oxidation and reduction takes place.
(i) Which of the following reactions involved the combination of two element?
(a) $\mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{2}$
(b) $4 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{SO}_{2}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{SO}_{3}$
(d) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}+\mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$
(ii) Consider the reaction
$\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Al} \rightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Fe}$
The above reaction is an example of
(a) combination reaction
(b) double displacement reaction
(c) decomposition reaction
(d) simple displacement reaction
(iii) The equation
$\mathrm{Mg}(s)+\mathrm{CuO}(s) \square \rightarrow \mathrm{MgO}(s)+\mathrm{Cu}(s)$ represents
(i) decomposition reaction
(ii) displacement reaction
(iii) combination reaction
(iv) double displacement reaction
(v) redox reaction
(a) (i) and (ii)
(b) (iii) and (iv)
(iv) and (v)
(c) (ii) and (v)
(d)
(iv) Which of the following is a decomposition reaction?
(a) $2 \mathrm{HgO} \xrightarrow{\text { heat }} 2 \mathrm{Hg}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3} \xrightarrow{\text { heat }} \mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow{\text { electrolysis }} \mathrm{H}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$
(d) All of these
(v) $\mathrm{CuO}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \square \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{Cu}$ reaction is an example of -
(a) redox reaction
(b) synthesis reaction
(c) neutralisation
(d) analysis reaction
examples.solution:
(i) (b)
(ii) (d) It is an example of displacement reaction. In it Al metal displaces iron from $\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$ when reaction is carried out in aqueous solution.
(111) (c) a .
b. Copper is displaced by Mg .
(iv) (d) As in all above reactions complex substances decomposes to give simple subtances.
(v) (a) As here $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ gets oxidised and CuO reduced to Cu .

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Questions — Q1
examples.problem: Why should a magnesium ribbon be cleaned before burning in air?
examples.solution: Magnesium ribbon has a coating of basic magnesium carbonate on its surface, which is formed by a slow action of moist air on it. Therefore before burning it in air, it is cleaned by a sand paper which removes the protective layer of basic magnesium carbonate from the surface of magnesium ribbon, so that it can easily burn.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Questions — Q2
examples.problem:
Write the balanced equation for the following chemical reactions.
(i) Hydrogen + Chlorine ⟶ Hydrogen chloride
(ii) Barium chloride + Aluminium sulphate ⟶ Barium sulphate + Aluminium chloride
(iii) Sodium + Water ⟶
Sodium hydroxide + Hydrogen
examples.solution:
(i) $\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{g})$
(ii) $3 \mathrm{BaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Al}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow$
$$
3 \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})
$$
(iii) $2 \mathrm{Na}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Questions — Q3
examples.problem:
Write a balanced chemical equation with state symbols for the following reactions.
(i) Solutions of barium chloride and sodium sulphate in water react to give insoluble barium sulphate and the solution of sodium chloride.
(ii) Sodium hydroxide solution (in water) reacts with hydrochloric acid solution (in water) to produce sodium chloride solution and water.
examples.solution:
(i) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow$
$$
\mathrm{BaSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq})
$$
(ii) $\mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{aq})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Questions — Q4
examples.problem:
A solution of a substance ' X ' is used for white washing.
(i) Name the substance ' X ' and write its formula.
(ii) Write the reaction of the substance ' X ' with water.
examples.solution:
(i) Quick lime- CaO .
(ii) $\mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}(\mathrm{aq})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Questions — Q5
examples.problem: Why is the amount of gas collected in one of the test tube in activity 1.7 of your NCERT text book double of the amount collected in the other? Name this gas.
examples.solution: The gases formed at two electrodes are produced due to decomposition of water on passing electricity. Since the electrolysis of water produces 2 volume of hydrogen gas and 1 volume of oxygen gas, thus we can say that the ratio of hydrogen and oxygen in water is $2: 1$ by volume and hence the amount of gas collected in one test tube will be double of the amount collected in other.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Questions — Q6
examples.problem: Why does the colour of copper sulphate solution changes when an iron nail is dipped in it?
examples.solution:
As iron is more reactive than copper thus it displaces copper from copper sulphate solution.
$$
\underset{\text { (Blue) }}{\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{~s})}+\underset{\text { (Green) }}{\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})} \longrightarrow \underset{(\mathrm{aq})}{\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})}+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{~s})
$$
In this way concentration of copper sulphate decreases while concentration of ferrous sulphate increases. As a result colour changes from blue to green.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Questions — Q7
examples.problem: Give an example of a double displacement reaction other than the one given in activity 1.10 of your NCERT text book.
examples.solution:
$\quad \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+3 \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{OH}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow$
Aluminium
Ammonium
chloride
hydroxide
$$
\underset{\substack{\text { Aluminium } \\ \text { hydroxide }}}{\mathrm{Al}(\mathrm{OH})_{3}(\mathrm{~s})}+\underset{\substack{\text { Ammonium } \\ \text { chloride }}}{3 \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}(\mathrm{aq})}
$$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Questions — Q8
examples.problem:
Identify the substances that are oxidised and the substances that are reduced in the following reactions.
(i) $4 \mathrm{Na}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s})$
(ii) $\mathrm{CuO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
examples.solution:
(i)
Therefore, Na is oxidised to $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$and $\mathrm{O}_{2}$ is reduced to $\mathrm{O}^{2-}$.
(ii)
Therefore, $\stackrel{+2}{\mathrm{CuO}}$ is reduced to Cu and $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ is oxidised to $\mathrm{H}^{+}$.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q1
examples.problem:
Which of the statements about the reaction below are incorrect?
$2 \mathrm{PbO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{~Pb}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(i) Lead is getting reduced.
(ii) Carbon dioxide is getting oxidised.
(iii) Carbon is getting oxidised.
(iv) Lead oxide is getting reduced.
(a) (i) and (ii)
(b) (i), (ii) and (iii)
(c) (i) and (iii)
(d) all
examples.solution: (a) The statements (i) and (ii) are incorrect because PbO is getting reduced, not Pb , similarly C is getting oxidized not $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q2
examples.problem:
$\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Al} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Fe}$
The above reaction is an example of a :
(a) combination reaction
(b) double displacement reaction
(c) decomposition reaction
(d) displacement reaction.
examples.solution: (d) The given reaction is an example of displacement reaction as in this reaction Al displaces Fe from $\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q3
examples.problem:
What happens when dilute hydrochloric acid is added to iron fillings? Tick the correct answer.
(a) Hydrogen gas and iron chloride are produced.
(b) Chlorine gas and iron hydroxide are produced.
(c) No reaction takes place.
(d) Iron salt and water are produced.
examples.solution: (a) $\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{FeCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q4
examples.problem: What is a balanced chemical equation? Why should chemical equations be balanced?
examples.solution:
Balanced chemical equation: - A chemical equation is said to be balanced if the number of atoms of each element participating in the reaction are equal on both
side of the equation. The chemical reaction should be balanced because law of conservation of mass states that 'In a chemical reaction total mass of the reactant must be equal to the total mass of the product'.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q5
examples.problem:
Translate the following statements into chemical equations and then balance them.
(a) Hydrogen gas combines with nitrogen to form ammonia.
(b) Hydrogen sulphide gas burns in air to give water and sulphur dioxide.
(c) Barium chloride reacts with aluminium sulphate to give aluminium chloride and a precipitate of barium sulphate.
(d) Potassium metal reacts with water to give potssium hydroxide and hydrogen gas.
examples.solution:
(a) $\mathrm{N}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(\mathrm{~g})$
(b) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+2 \mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(c) $3 \mathrm{BaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Al}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \longrightarrow$
$$
3 \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})
$$
(d) $2 \mathrm{~K}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q6
examples.problem:
Balance the following chemical equations.
(a) $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}+\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}+\mathrm{NaCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AgCl}+\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}$.
(d) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+\mathrm{HCl}$
examples.solution:
(a) $2 \mathrm{HNO}_{3}+\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $2 \mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}+\mathrm{NaCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AgCl}+\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}$
(d) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{HCl}$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q7
examples.problem:
Write the balanced chemical equations for the following reactions.
(a) Calcium hydroxide + Carbon dioxide ⟶
Calcium carbonate + water
(b) Zinc + Silver nitrate ⟶ Zinc nitrate + Silver
(c) Aluminium + Copper chloride ⟶
Aluminium chloride + Copper
(d) Barium chloride + Potassium sulphate ⟶
Barium sulphate + Potassium chloride
examples.solution:
(a) $\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}+\mathrm{CO}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{Zn}+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Zn}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+2 \mathrm{Ag}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{Al}+3 \mathrm{CuCl}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}+3 \mathrm{Cu}$
(d) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{KCl}$.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q8
examples.problem:
Write the balanced chemical equation for the following and identify the type of reaction in each case.
(a) Potassium bromide(aq) + Barium iodide(aq)
⟶ Potassium iodide(aq) + Barium bromide(s)
(b) Zinc carbonate(s) ⟶
Zinc oxide(s) + Carbon dioxide(s)
(c) Hydrogen(g) + Chlorine (g) ⟶
Hydrogen chloride(g)
(d) Magnesium(s) + Hydrochloric acid(aq)
⟶ Magnesium chloride(aq) + Hydrogen(g)
examples.solution:
(a) $2 \mathrm{KBr}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{BaI}_{2}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KI}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{BaBr}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})$
(b) $\mathrm{ZnCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(c) $\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{g})$
(d) $\mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{g})+2 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{MgCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q9
examples.problem: What does one mean by exothermic and endothermic reaction? Give examples.
examples.solution:
Exothermic reaction : - Those reactions which occur with the evolution of heat are exothermic reactions. e.g. $2 \mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \xrightarrow[\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\text { heat }]{ }$
Endothermic reaction : - Those reactions which occur with the absorption of heat are endothermic reactions. e.g.
$2 \mathrm{~Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{\text { heat }} 2 \mathrm{PbO}(\mathrm{s})+4 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q10
examples.problem: Why is respiration considered an exothermic reaction? Explain.
examples.solution:
Respiration is the most important biochemical reaction which releases energy in the cell. When we breathe air (oxygen) enters into our lungs and oxygen binds itself to haemoglobin present in red blood cells (RBC). It is then carried to million of cells present in our body. Respiration occurs in these cells and is accompanied by the combustion of glucose $\left(\mathrm{C}_{6} \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}_{6}\right)$ producing carbon dioxide ( $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$ ) and water ( $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ ). A large amount of energy is released in this chemical reaction.
$$
6 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+6 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+2820 \mathrm{KJ}
$$
Since the process is accompanied by release of energy so it is considered as an exothermic reaction.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q11
examples.problem: Why are decomposition reactions called the opposite of combination reactions? Write equations for these reactions.
examples.solution:
During decomposition reaction a single reactant decompose to form two or more products, whereas in combination reaction two or more reactants react to form a single product. e.g.
$\mathrm{N}_{2}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}$ (Combination reaction)
$2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{H}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$ (Decomposition reaction)

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q12
examples.problem: What is the difference between displacement and double displacement reactions? Write equations for these reactions.
examples.solution:
Displacement reaction : - In this reaction an element which is more reactive displaces the other element which is less reactive from its salt solution. e.g.
$\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})$
Double displacement reaction : - In this reaction exchange of ions between two reactants takes place. e.g.
$\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q13
examples.problem: In the refining of silver, the recovery of silver from silver nitrate solution involved displacement by copper metal. Write down the reaction involved.
examples.solution: $\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q14
examples.problem: What do you mean by a precipitation reaction? Explain by giving examples.
examples.solution: Those reactions which produce an insoluble product or precipitate are known as precipitation reaction. e.g. $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{s}) \downarrow+\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q15
examples.problem: A shiny brown coloured element ' $X$ ' on heating in air becomes black in colour. Name the element ' X ' and the black coloured compound formed.
examples.solution: $\underset{\text { brown }}{2 \mathrm{Cu}}+\underset{\text { from air }}{\mathrm{O}_{2}} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{\text { Cuprous Oxide }}{2 \mathrm{CuO}}$ (Black)

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q16
examples.problem: Why do we apply paint on iron articles?
examples.solution: To prevent iron articles from rusting, we apply paint on it, by which iron does not come in contact with air and moisture which causes rusting.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q17
examples.problem: Oil and fat containing food items are flushed with nitrogen. Why?
examples.solution: When the oil and fat containing food is surrounded by unreactive gas nitrogen, oxygen become unavailable to cause oxidation and therefore prevent rancidity.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q18
examples.problem:
Explain the following terms with one example each.
(a) Corrosion
(b) Rancidity
examples.solution:
Corrosion : - It is a process in which metals are eaten up gradually by the action of air, moisture or a chemical on their surface. For example rusting of iron.
Rancidity - When fat and oil containing food is oxidised with air or oxygen, their smell and taste changes. This process is called rancidity.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text - Book Exercise — Q19
examples.problem: Write chemical equation for decomposition reactions where energy is supplied in the form of (i) heat (ii) light (iii) electricity.
examples.solution:
(i) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{\text { heat }} \mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(ii) $2 \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{s}) \xrightarrow{\text { sunlight }} 2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(iii) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \xrightarrow{\text { electricity }} 2 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Exemplar Questions — Q1
examples.problem:
Complete the missing components/variables given as $x$ and $y$ in the following reactions
(i) $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{KI}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbI}_{2}(x)+\mathrm{KNO}_{3}(y)$
(ii) $\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+x(s)$
(iii) $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}(x)+\mathrm{H}_{2}(y)$
(iv) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{x} \mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
examples.solution:
(i) $x \rightarrow(\mathrm{~s})$
$$
y \rightarrow(\mathrm{aq})
$$
(ii) $x \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}$
(iii) $x \rightarrow(\mathrm{aq}), y \rightarrow(\mathrm{~g})$
(iv) $x \rightarrow$ Heat

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Exemplar Questions — Q2
examples.problem: Grapes hanging on the plant do not ferment but after being plucked from the plant can be fermented. Under what conditions do these grapes ferment? Is it a chemical or a physical change?
examples.solution: Grapes when attached to the plants are living and therefore their own immune system prevents fermentation. The microbes can grow in the plucked grapes and under anaerobic conditions these can be fermented. This is a chemical change.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Exemplar Questions — Q3
examples.problem:
During the reaction of some metals with dilute hydrochloric acid, following observations were made.
(i) Silver metal does not show any change
(ii) The temperature of the reaction mixture rises when aluminium (Al) is added.
(iii) The reaction of sodium metal is found to be highly explosive.
(iv) Some bubbles of a gas are seen when lead ( Pb ) is reacted with the acid.
Explain these observations giving suitable reasons.
examples.solution:
(i) Silver metal does not react with dilute HCl .
(ii) The temperature of the reaction mixture rises when aluminium is added because it is an exothermic reaction.
(iii) Reaction of sodium metal is found to be highly explosive because it is an exothermic reaction.
(iv) When lead is treated with hydrochloric acid, bubbles of hydrogen gas are evolved.
$\mathrm{Pb}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{PbCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2}$

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Exemplar Questions — Q4
examples.problem: Ferrous sulphate decomposes with the evolution of a gas having a characteristic odour of burning sulphur. Write the chemical reaction involved and identify the type of reaction.
examples.solution: $2 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{\text { Heat }} \mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{SO}_{3}(\mathrm{~g})$ It is a thermal decomposition reaction.

examples.title: Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Exemplar Questions — Q5
examples.problem: You are provided with two containers made up of copper and aluminium. You are also provided with solutions of dilute HCl , dilute $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}, \mathrm{ZnCl}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$. In which of the above containers these solutions can be kept?
examples.solution:
(A) When solutions are kept in copper container.
(i) Dilute HCl
Copper does not react with dilute HCl . Therefore, it can be kept.
(ii) Dilute $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}$
Nitric acid acts as a strong oxidising agent and reacts with copper vessel, therefore cannot be kept.
(iii) $\mathrm{ZnCl}_{2}$
Zinc is more reactive than copper ( Cu ) therefore, no displacement reaction occurs and hence can be kept.
(iv) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
Copper does not react with water. Therefore, can be kept.
(B) When solutions are kept in aluminium containers.
(i) Dilute HCl
Aluminium reacts with dilute HCl to form its salt and hydrogen is evolved. Therefore, cannot be kept.
$2 \mathrm{Al}+6 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}$
(ii) Dilute $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}$
Aluminium gets oxidised by dilute $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}$ to form a layer of $\mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$ and cannot be kept.
(iii) $\mathrm{ZnCl}_{2}$
Aluminium being more reactive than zinc can displace zinc ion from the solution. Therefore, the solution cannot be kept.
$2 \mathrm{Al}+3 \mathrm{ZnCl}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}+3 \mathrm{Zn}$
(iv) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
Aluminium does not react with cold or hot water.
Therefore, water can be kept.
Aluminium is attacked by steam to form aluminium oxide and hydrogen gas.
$2 \mathrm{~A} 1(\mathrm{~s})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
Exercise 3

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q1
examples.problem:
Which of the following reactions involves the combination of two elements?
(a) $\mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$
(b) $4 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{SO}_{2}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{SO}_{3}$
(d) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}+\mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$
examples.solution:
(b) Except (b) all other reactions involve compounds.
$$
\underset{\substack{\text { (blue) } \\ \text { (double decomposition reaction) }}}{\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \underset{\substack{\text { (black) } \\ \mathrm{CuS}}}{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}}
$$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q2
examples.problem:
When hydrogen sulphide gas is passed through a blue solution of copper sulphate, a black precipitate of copper sulphide is obtained and the sulphuric acid so formed remains in the solution. The reaction is an example of -
(a) a combination reaction
(b) a displacement reaction
(c) a decomposition reaction
(d) a double decomposition reaction

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q3
examples.problem:
What happens when copper rod is dipped in iron sulphate solution?
(a) Copper displaces iron
(b) Blue colour of copper sulphate solution is obtained
(c) No reaction takes place
(d) Reaction is exothermic
examples.solution: (c) Iron is more reactive than copper, hence Cu will not displace iron from iron sulphate, hence no reaction will take place.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q4
examples.problem:
A student added dilute HCl to a test tube containing zinc granules and made following observations which one is correct?
(a) The zinc surface became dull and black.
(b) A gas evolved which burns with a pop sound.
(c) The solution remained colourless.
(d) The solution becomes green in colour.
examples.solution:
40 g CO is formed from $\frac{100}{44} \times 44=10 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$
If mass of $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$ is 10 g , then weight of empty test tube $=30.08-10.0=20.08 \mathrm{~g}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q5
examples.problem:
A dilute solution of sodium carbonate was added to two test tubes (A) containing dil HCl and (B) containing dilute NaOH . The correct observation was -
(a) a brown coloured gas liberated in test tube A.
(b) a brown coloured gas liberated in test tube B.
(c) a colourless gas liberated in test tube A.
(d) a colourless gas liberated in test tube B.
examples.solution:
(c) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
$\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}+\mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow$ no reaction

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q6
examples.problem:
A balanced chemical equation is in accordance with -
(a) Avogadro's law
(b) law of multiple proportion
(c) law of conservation of mass
(d) law of gaseous volumes.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q7
examples.problem:
The equation
$$
\mathrm{Cu}+x \mathrm{HNO}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+y \mathrm{NO}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}
$$
The values of $x$ and $y$ are -
(a) 3 and 5
(b) 8 and 6
(c) 4 and 2
(d) 7 and 1
examples.solution: (c) $\mathrm{Cu}+4 \mathrm{HNO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q8
examples.problem:
$\mathrm{Zn}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}($ dil $) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \uparrow$ Above reaction is -
(a) decomposition reaction
(b) single displacement reaction
(c) combination reaction
(d) synthesis reaction

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q9
examples.problem:
The reaction in which two compounds exchange their ions to form two new compounds is -
(a) a displacement reaction
(b) a decomposition reaction
(c) an isomerization reaction
(d) a double displacement reaction
examples.solution:
The oxidation number of Mn in $\mathrm{KMnO}_{4}$ is-
(a) +7
(b) -7
(c) +1
(d) -1

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q10
examples.problem:
When the gases sulphur dioxide and hydrogen sulphide mix in the presence of water, the reaction is $\mathrm{SO}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+3 \mathrm{~S}$. Here hydrogen sulphide is acting as -
(a) an oxidising agent
(b) a reducing agent
(c) a dehydrating agent
(d) a catalyst
examples.solution: (b) Here $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}$ is behaves as a reducing agent and oxidises to $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q11
examples.problem:
$\mathrm{CuO}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{Cu}$, reaction is an example of -
(a) redox reaction
(b) synthesis reaction
(c) neutralisation
(d) analysis reaction
examples.solution: (a) $\underset{\text { Reduction }}{\mathrm{CuO}+\underset{\sim}{\mathrm{H}_{2}} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}+\underset{\sim}{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}}}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q12
examples.problem:
A substance which oxidises itself and reduces other is known as -
(a) oxidising agent
(b) reducing agent
(c) both of these
(d) none of these

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q13
examples.problem:
A redox reaction is one in which -
(a) both the substances are reduced.
(b) both the substances are oxidised.
(c) an acid is neutralised by the base.
(d) one substance is oxidised while the other is reduced.
examples.solution:
In which of the following reactions, $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}$ acts as a reducing agent?
(a) $2 \mathrm{KI}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KOH}+\mathrm{I}_{2}$
(b) $2 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{PbS}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbSO}_{4}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{Ag}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q14
examples.problem:
In the following equations:
$\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}+x \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$, the value of $x$ is-
(a) 1
(b) 2
(c) 3
(d) 4
examples.solution: (b) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q15
examples.problem:
In the equation, $\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{HNO}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{NaNO}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ nitric acid is acting as -
(a) an oxidising agent
(b) an acid
(c) a nitrating agent
(d) a dehydrating agent
examples.solution: (b) The reaction represents a neutralisation reaction in which base $(\mathrm{NaOH})$ reacts with an acid $\left(\mathrm{HNO}_{3}\right)$ to form salt $\left(\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}\right)$ and water $\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)$.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q16
examples.problem:
$\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Al} \rightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Fe}$
The above reaction is an example of $\mathrm{a}-$
(a) combination reaction
(b) double displacement reaction
(c) decomposition reaction
(d) displacement reaction

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q17
examples.problem:
White silver chloride in sunlight turns to -
(a) grey
(b) yellow
(c) remain white
(d) red
examples.solution: (a) White silver chloride in sunlight turns to grey.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q18
examples.problem:
Black and white photography uses -
(a) decomposition of silver chloride.
(b) decomposition of silver bromide.
(c) both
(d) none of these

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q19
examples.problem:
When copper powder is heated it gets coated with -
(a) black copper oxide
(b) yellow copper oxide
(c) red copper oxide
(d) None of these
examples.solution: (a) $2 \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{\text { Black }}{2 \mathrm{CuO}}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q20
examples.problem:
Combination of phosphorus and oxygen is an example of-
(a) oxidation
(b) reduction
(c) rancidity
(d) None of these
examples.solution:
(a) $4 \mathrm{P}+3 \mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow{\text { (Oxidation) }} 2 \mathrm{P}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$
$4 \mathrm{P}+5 \mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow{\text { (Oxidation) }} 2 \mathrm{P}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q21
examples.problem:
Rusting of iron is an example of -
(a) reduction
(b) redox
(c) oxidation
(d) dissociation
examples.solution: (b) 2 Fe (s) $+\mathrm{O}_{2}$ (g) $+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}$(aq) $\longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ (aq) $+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ (l)

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q22
examples.problem:
Which of the following does not corrode when exposed to the atmosphere?
(a) Iron
(b) Copper
(c) Gold
(d) Silver
examples.solution: (c) Gold is least reactive hence does not corrode at all.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q23
examples.problem:
Take about $1.0 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$ in a test tube. Heat it over a flame, a colourless gas comes out. The reaction is called a
(a) decomposition reaction
(b) displacement reaction
(c) double decomposition reaction
(d) double displacement reaction
examples.solution: (a) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q24
examples.problem:
Hydrogen sulphide $\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}\right)$ is a strong reducing agent. Which of the following reactions shows its reducing action?
(a) $\mathrm{Cd}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CdS}+2 \mathrm{HNO}_{3}$
(b) $\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CuS}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{FeCl}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{FeCl}_{2}+2 \mathrm{HCl}+\mathrm{S}$
(d) $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbS}+2 \mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}$
examples.solution:
(c) $\underset{\text { Oxidation }}{\mathrm{FeCl}_{3}}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \underset{\sim}{\mathrm{~S}}$
In the given reaction $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}$ undergoes oxidation, hence behave as a reducing agent.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q25
examples.problem:
$2 \mathrm{CuI} \rightarrow \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{CuI}_{2}$, the reaction is -
(a) redox
(b) neutralisation
(c) oxidation
(d) reduction
examples.solution:
(a)
Oxidation and reduction both occur so the reaction is redox.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q26
examples.problem:
When copper turnings are added to silver nitrate solution, a blue coloured solution is formed after some time. It is because, copper -
(a) displaces silver from the solution
(b) forms a blue coloured complex with $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$
(c) is oxidised to $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$
(d) is reduced to $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$
examples.solution: (a) Cu is more reactive than Ag .

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q27
examples.problem:
$\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})$. This is -
(a) oxidation
(b) reduction
(c) redox reaction
(d) none of these
examples.solution: (b) $\stackrel{+2}{\mathrm{Zn}}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})$; reduction

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q28
examples.problem:
A substance A reacts with another substance B to produce the product C and a gas D . If a mixture of the gas D and ammonia is passed through an aqueous solution of C , baking soda is formed. The substances A and B are
[NTSE]
(a) HCl and NaOH
(b) HCl and $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}$
(c) Na and HCl
(d) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
examples.solution:
(b)
$\mathrm{CO}_{2}+\mathrm{NH}_{3}+\mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Baking soda }}{\mathrm{NaHCO}_{3}}+\mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$
Hence $\mathrm{A} \& \mathrm{~B}$ are HCl and $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q29
examples.problem:
Chemically the 'water gas' is
[JSTSE]
(a) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ (gaseous)
(b) $\mathrm{CO}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{CH}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{H}_{2}$
examples.solution: (d) Water gas → $\mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{H}_{2}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q30
examples.problem:
The oxidation number of sulphur is -4 in
[KVPY]
(a) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}$
(b) $\mathrm{CS}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$
(d) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{3}$
examples.solution:
(*) Let the oxidation state of S be $x$
(i) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore 2 & +x=0 \\
& x=-2
\end{aligned}
$$
(ii) $\mathrm{CS}_{2}$
$$
4+2 x=0 \Rightarrow x=-2
$$
(iii) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2(+1)+x+4(-2)=0 \\
& 2+x-8=0 \\
& x=+6
\end{aligned}
$$
(iv) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{3}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2(+1)+x+3(-2)=0 \\
& 2+x-6=0 \\
& x=+4
\end{aligned}
$$
None of these, option is correct.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q31
examples.problem:
Identify the endothermic process from the following
[JSTSE]
(a) Addition of conc. HCl to water
(b) $\mathrm{CH}_{4}(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
(c) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g})$
(d) $\mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}(\mathrm{aq})$
examples.solution: (c) Conversion of liquid to gas is endothermic process.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q32
examples.problem:
The schematic diagram is given below
[NTSE]
Which of the following is a correct statement?
(a) A and E are chemically same.
(b) A and D are chemically same.
(c) D and E are chemically same.
(d) C and E are chemically same.
examples.solution:
(b)
$\mathrm{A}=\mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl} ; \mathrm{D}=\mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$
Hence correct statement is: A and D are chemically same.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q33
examples.problem:
The oxidation states of P atom in $\mathrm{POCl}_{3}, \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{PO}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{P}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{6}$, respectively are
[KVPY]
(a) $+5,+4,+4$
(b) $\quad+5,+5,+4$
(c) $+4,+4,+5$
(d) $+3,+4,+5$
examples.solution:
(a) Let the oxidation state of P -atom in $\mathrm{POCl}_{3}, \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{PO}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{H}_{4} \mathrm{P}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{6}$ be $x$.
(i) $\mathrm{POCl}_{3}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x+1(-2)+3(-1)=0 \\
& x-2-3=0 \\
& \quad x=+5
\end{aligned}
$$
(ii) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{PO}_{3}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2(1)+x+3(-2)=0 \\
& 2+x-6=0 \\
& \quad x=+4
\end{aligned}
$$
(iii) $\mathrm{H}_{4} \mathrm{P}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{6}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 4(1)+2 x+6(-2)=0 \\
& 4+2 x-12=0 \\
& 2 x=8 \\
& x=+4
\end{aligned}
$$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q34
examples.problem:
The process of respiration is :
[JSTSE]
(a) Oxidation reaction which is endothermic
(b) Reduction reaction which is endothermic
(c) Combination reaction which is exothermic
(d) Oxidation reaction which is exothermic
examples.solution: (d) Respiration is oxidation and exothermic process.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q35

examples.solution: (d) Layer of silver sulphide deposited on the silver articles when exposed to air.

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q36
examples.problem:
A test tube along with calcium carbonate in it initially weighed 30.08 g . A heating experiment was performed on this test tube till calcium carbonate completely decomposed with evolution of a gas. Loss of weight during this experiment was 4.40 g . What is the weight of the empty test tube in this experiment?
(a) 20.08 g
(b) 21.00 g
(c) 24.50 g
(d) 2.008 g
examples.solution:
(a) On thermal decomposition of calcium carbonate
$$
\underset{100 \mathrm{~g}}{\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{56 \mathrm{~g}}{\mathrm{CaO}}+\underset{44 \mathrm{~g}}{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}
$$
$44 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{CO}_{2}$ is formed from $100 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q37
examples.problem:

[NTSE]
examples.solution: (d) A-III, B-I, C-II, D-IV

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q38
examples.problem:
If we added $\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}$ to above four test tubes, in which test tube we observe black residue?
[NTSE]
(a) "A" and "B"
(b) "B" and "C"
(c) "A" and "C"
(d) " B " and " D "
examples.solution:
(d) Zn and Al are more reactive than iron, therefore they will displace iron from its salt solution giving black residue, while Cu being less reactive than iron will not able to displace iron from its salt solution.
$\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{Al} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}+3 \mathrm{Fe}$
$\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Zn} \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Fe}$
$\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Cu} \longrightarrow$ No reaction
$\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Fe} \longrightarrow$ No reaction

examples.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions — Q1
examples.problem:
Which of the following statements is correct?
(a) A chemical equation tells us about the substances involved in a reaction.
(b) A chemical equation informs us about the symbols and formula of substances involved in a reaction.
(c) A chemical equation tells us about the atom or molecules of the reactants and products involved in a reaction.
(d) A chemical equation represents energy changes during a reaction.
examples.solution:
(b) Except (b) all other reactions involve compounds.
$$
\underset{\substack{\text { (blue) } \\ \text { (double decomposition reaction) }}}{\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \underset{\substack{\text { (black) } \\ \mathrm{CuS}}}{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}}
$$

examples.solution: (c) Iron is more reactive than copper, hence Cu will not displace iron from iron sulphate, hence no reaction will take place.

examples.solution:

examples.solution: (b) The reaction represents a neutralisation reaction in which base $(\mathrm{NaOH})$ reacts with an acid $\left(\mathrm{HNO}_{3}\right)$ to form salt $\left(\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}\right)$ and water $\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)$.

examples.problem:

\underset{\mathrm{X}}{\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{~s})}+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}(\mathrm{q})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{~s})

$$
\mathrm{Au}<\mathrm{Ag}<\mathrm{H}<\mathrm{Pb}<\mathrm{Ni}<\mathrm{Fe}<\mathrm{Cr}<\mathrm{Na}<\mathrm{K}
$$

Let O.N. of Os be $x$ then $1 \times(x)+4(-2)=0$
$\therefore x=8$

(d) $\mathrm{CrO}_{5}$
examples.solution: (a) $\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CO})_{5}$ is metal carbonyl, hence O.N. of Fe is zero.

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q8
examples.problem:
Oxidation number of S in $\mathrm{S}^{2-}$ is-
(a) -2
(b) 0
(c) -6
(d) +6
examples.solution:
(a) Charge on an atom is called its oxidation number.
So, oxidation no. of $\mathrm{S}^{2-}$ is -2 .

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q9
examples.problem:
The oxidation number of Mn in $\mathrm{KMnO}_{4}$ is-
(a) +7
(b) -7
(c) +1
(d) -1
examples.solution: (a) $1+\mathrm{x}+4 \times(-2)=0 ; \mathrm{x}=+7$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q10
examples.problem:
Oxidation number of oxygen atom in $\mathrm{O}_{2}$ molecule is-
(a) 0
(b) -2
(c) +2
(d) $-\frac{1}{2}$
examples.solution: (a) Ox. No. of an element in uncombined state $=0$.

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q11
examples.problem:
The oxidation number of N in $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ is-
(a) +3
(b) +5
(c) -3
(d) 0
examples.solution:
(c) Let oxidation number of N in $\mathrm{NH}_{3}=x$
$$
\therefore \quad x+(+1 \times 3)=0 \quad \text { or } \quad x=-3
$$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q12
examples.problem:
Oxygen has an oxidation state of +2 in the compound-
(a) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{F}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
examples.solution:
(d) In $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}: \Rightarrow 2 \times(+1)+2 \times x=0 \Rightarrow x=-1$
In $\mathrm{CO}_{2}: \Rightarrow 4+2 x=0 \Rightarrow x=-2$
In $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}: \Rightarrow 2 \times(+1)+x=0 \Rightarrow x=-2$
In $\mathrm{F}_{2} \mathrm{O}: \Rightarrow 2 \times(-1)+x=0 \Rightarrow x=+2$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q13
examples.problem:
In which of the following reactions, $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}$ acts as a reducing agent?
(a) $2 \mathrm{KI}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KOH}+\mathrm{I}_{2}$
(b) $2 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{PbS}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbSO}_{4}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{Ag}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$
examples.solution: (d) In (a), (b) and (c) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}$ acts as oxidising agent.

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q14
examples.problem:
In a balance equation $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+x \mathrm{HI} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}+y \mathrm{I}_{2}+z \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$, the values of $x, y, z$ are-
(a) $x=3, y=5, z=2$
(b) $x=4, y=8, z=5$
(c) $x=8, y=4, z=4$
(d) $x=5, y=3, z=4$
examples.solution:
(c) The value of $x, y, z$ are $8,4,4$ respectively hence the reaction is
$$
\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+8 \mathrm{HI} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}+4 \mathrm{I}_{2}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}
$$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q15
examples.problem:
Consider the following reaction :
$x \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{6}(\mathrm{~g})+y \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow m \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+n \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
Which of the following set of coefficients balances the above redox reaction?
| | $x$ | $y$ | $m$ | $n$ |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| (a) | 1 | 3 | 2 | 2 |
| (b) | 2 | 7 | 4 | 6 |
| (c) | 2 | 3 | 2 | 2 |
| (d) | 1 | 7 | 2 | 3 |
examples.solution: (b) $2 \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{6}(\mathrm{~g})+7 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+6 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q16
examples.problem:
What will be the value of $x, y$ and $z$ in following equation?
$\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}+x \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow y \mathrm{CO}_{2}+z \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(a) $x=1, y=2, z=2$
(b) $x=2, y=2, z=2$
(c) $x=2, y=2, z=1$
(d) $x=1, y=2, z=4$
examples.solution:
(a) Balanced equation is :
$$
\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{CO}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}
$$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q17
examples.problem:
Which of the following reactions is an example of a redox reaction?
[NTSE]
(a) $\mathrm{XeF}_{4}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \mathrm{~F}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{XeF}_{6}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{XeF}_{2}+\mathrm{PF}_{5} \longrightarrow\left[\mathrm{XeF}^{+} \mathrm{PF}_{6}^{-}\right.$
(c) $\mathrm{XeF}_{6}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{XeOF}_{4}+2 \mathrm{HF}$
(d) $\mathrm{XeF}_{6}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{XeO}_{2} \mathrm{~F}_{2}+4 \mathrm{HF}$
examples.solution:
(a) In the reaction
$$
\stackrel{+4}{\mathrm{XeF}_{4}+\stackrel{\text { Oxidation }}{+1} \underset{\text { Reduction }}{\mathrm{O}_{2} \mathrm{~F}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{XeF}_{6}}+\stackrel{0}{\mathrm{O}_{2}}}
$$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q18
examples.problem:
The formal oxidation numbers of Cr and Cl in the ions $\mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}^{2-}$ and $\mathrm{ClO}_{3}{ }^{-}$, respectively are
[KVPY 2018]
(a) +6 and +7
(b) +7 and +5
(c) +6 and +5
(d) +8 and +7
examples.solution:
(c) In $\mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}^{3-}$, let the oxidation state of Cr be $x$.
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad 2(x)+7(-2) & =-2 \\
2 x-14 & =-2 \\
2 x & =12 \\
x & =+6
\end{aligned}
$$
In $\mathrm{ClO}^{-}$, let the oxidation state of Cl be $x$.
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore 1(x)+3(-2) & =-1 \\
x-6 & =-1 \\
x & =+5
\end{aligned}
$$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q19
examples.problem:
The oxidation states of Cr in $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right] \mathrm{Cl}_{3},\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{C}_{6} \mathrm{H}_{6}\right)_{2}\right]$, and $\mathrm{K}_{2}\left[\mathrm{Cr}(\mathrm{CN})_{2}(\mathrm{O})_{2}(\mathrm{O})_{2}\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)\right]$ respectively are
[NTSE]
(a) $+3,+4$, and +6
(b) +3, +2, and +4
(c) $\quad+3,0$, and +6
(d) $+3,0$, and +4
examples.solution:
(c) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right] \mathrm{Cl}_{3}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow x+6 \times 0+(-1) \times 3=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x=+3 \\
& {\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{C}_{6} \mathrm{H}_{6}\right)_{2}\right]} \\
& x+2 \times 0=0 ; x=0 \\
& \mathrm{~K}_{2}\left[\mathrm{Cr}(\mathrm{CN})_{2}(\mathrm{O})_{2}\left(\mathrm{O}_{2}\right)\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)\right] \\
& 2 \times 1+x+2 \times(-1)+2 \times(-2)+(-2)+0=0 \\
& x=+6 \\
& +5 \quad+2 \quad 0 \quad-3
\end{aligned}
$$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q20
examples.problem:
The correct order of N-compounds in its decreasing order of oxidation states is
[JSTSE]
(a) $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}, \mathrm{NO}, \mathrm{N}_{2}, \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$
(b) $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}, \mathrm{NO}, \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{N}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{N}_{2}, \mathrm{NO}, \mathrm{HNO}_{3}$
(d) $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}, \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{NO}, \mathrm{N}_{2}$
examples.solution: (a) $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}, \mathrm{NO}, \mathrm{N}_{2}, \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q21
examples.problem:
For the redox reaction
$\mathrm{MnO}_{4}^{-}+\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}^{2-}+\mathrm{H}^{+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
The correct coefficients of the reactants for the balanced equation are
[JSTSE]
| | $\mathrm{MnO}_{4}^{-}$ | $\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}^{2-}$ | $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| (a) | 16 | 5 | 2 |
| (b) | 2 | 5 | 16 |
| (c) | 5 | 16 | 2 |
| (d) | 2 | 16 | 5 |

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q22
examples.problem:
The correct order of the oxidation states of nitrogen in NO , $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{NO}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$ is:
[NTSE]
(a) $\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{NO}<\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}<\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}<\mathrm{NO}<\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}<\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}<\mathrm{NO}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}$
(d) $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}<\mathrm{NO}<\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}$
examples.solution:
| (d) (Oxide) | (Oxidation state) |
| :--- | :---: |
| $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ | +1 |
| NO | +2 |
| $\mathrm{~N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$ | +3 |
| $\mathrm{NO}_{2}$ | +4 |
So, $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}<\mathrm{NO}<\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}$

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q23
examples.problem:
What is the change in oxidation number of carbon in the following reaction?
[JSTSE]
$\mathrm{CH}_{4}(g)+4 \mathrm{Cl}_{2}(g) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CCl}_{4}(l)+4 \mathrm{HCl}(g)$
(a) 0 to +4
(b) -4 to +4
(c) 0 to - 4
(d) +4 to +4
examples.solution:
(b) $\stackrel{-4}{\mathrm{CH}_{4}}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \stackrel{+4}{\mathrm{CCl}_{4}}(l)+4 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{g})$
Change in oxidation state of carbon is -4 to +4 .

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q24
examples.problem:
In which of the following pairs, there is zero difference in the oxidation number of the underlined elements?
(a) $\mathrm{NO}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}$
(b) $\underline{\mathrm{P}}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}$ and $\underline{\mathrm{P}}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{10}$
(c) $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ and NO
(d) $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$
examples.solution:
(a, b) O.N. of N in $\mathrm{NO}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}$ is +4
∴ difference is zero.
O.N. of P in $\mathrm{P}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}$ and $\mathrm{P}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{10}$ is $+5 \therefore$ difference is zero
O.N. of $\mathrm{NinN}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ is +1 and in NO is +2 . The difference is 1
O.N. of $\mathrm{SinSO}_{2}$ is +4 and in $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$ is +6 . The difference is +2 .

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q25
examples.problem:
Which of the following reactions is/are not redox reaction?
(a) $\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{CaCl}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{MgSO}_{4} \rightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+\mathrm{MgCl}_{2}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{~S}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}^{2-}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \rightarrow 4 \mathrm{SO}_{4}^{2-}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}$
(d) $\mathrm{Cu}_{2} \mathrm{~S}+2 \mathrm{FeO} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cu}+2 \mathrm{Fe}+\mathrm{SO}_{2}$
examples.solution:
( $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c})$ In redox reactions oxidation and reduction take place side by side.
$\mathrm{Cu}_{2} \mathrm{~S}+2 \mathrm{FeO} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cu}+2 \mathrm{Fe}+\mathrm{SO}_{2}$.
O.N. of Cu changes from +1 to 0 (reduction) and O.N. of S changes from -2 to +4 (oxidation). Hence (d) is redox reaction.

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q26
examples.problem:
The oxidation number of an element in a compound is evaluated on the basis of certian rules. Which of the following rules is/are correct in this respect?
(a) The oxidation number of hydrogen is always +1 .
(b) The algebraic sum of all the oxidation numbers in a compound is zero.
(c) An element in the free or the uncombined state bears oxidation number zero.
(d) In all its compounds, the oxidation number of fluorine is -1 .
examples.solution: (b, c, d)

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q27
examples.problem:
Which one of the following reaction(s) involve either oxidation or reduction?
(a) $\quad \mathrm{VO}_{2}^{+} \rightarrow \mathrm{V}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$
(b) $\mathrm{Na} \rightarrow \mathrm{Na}^{+}$
(c) $\mathrm{CrO}_{4}^{2-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}^{2-}$
(d) $\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}$
examples.solution:
$(\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{d}) \stackrel{+5}{\mathrm{~V}} \mathrm{O}_{2}^{+} \longrightarrow \stackrel{+3}{\mathrm{~V}} \mathrm{O}_{3}$ (Reduction)
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\mathrm{Na} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}^{+} \text {(Oxidation) } & \\
\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Zn} \text { (Reduction) } & \\
* & \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}^{2-} \\
\mathrm{CrO}_{4}^{2-} & 2 x+(-2) \times 7=-2 \\
x+[(-2) \times 4]=-2 & 2 x=14-2=12, \\
x=8-2=+6 & x=\frac{12}{2}=+6
\end{array}
$$
In this reaction oxidation and reduction are not involved because there is no change in oxidation number.

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q28
examples.problem:
Which of the following is/are not redox reaction(s)?
(a) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{HCl}$
(b) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}+\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{5} \mathrm{OH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOC}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{5}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $2 \mathrm{FeCl}_{3}+\mathrm{SnCl}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{FeCl}_{2}+\mathrm{SnCl}_{4}$
examples.solution:
(a, b, c) The reaction in which both oxidation and reduction take place simultaneously is called redox reaction.
Therefore only (d) is redox reaction.

examples.title: Integer/Numerical Value Type Questions — Directions — Q29
examples.problem:
Choose the correct option(s) for the following statement:
When $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ changes to $\mathrm{Sn}^{4+}$ in a reaction then
(a) $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ loses two electrons.
(b) $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ gains two electrons.
(c) $\mathrm{Sn}^{4+}$ loses two electrons.
(d) This is an example of oxidation reaction.
examples.solution:
(a, d) $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+} \xrightarrow{-2 \mathrm{e}^{-}} \mathrm{Sn}^{4+}$
In this reaction $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ changes to $\mathrm{Sn}^{4+}$ and this represents oxidation reaction.

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — Directions — Q30
examples.problem:
Assertion : In a reaction
$\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})$
Zn is a reductant but itself gets oxidized.
Reason : In a redox reaction, oxidant is reduced by accepting electrons and reductant is oxidized by losing electrons.
examples.solution:
(a) Both assertion and reason are true and
reason is the correct explanation of assertion

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — Directions — Q31
examples.problem:
Assertion : $\mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{F}_{2}(\mathrm{~s}) \rightarrow \mathrm{MgF}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})$, magnesium loses electrons and acts as a reducing agent.
Reason : Reduction in general means acceptance of electron(s) by a reactant.
examples.solution:
(b) Both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
In general, oxidation is the loss of electrons and the reactant like magnesium that loses electron acts as a reductant or reducing agent.

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — Directions — Q32
examples.problem:
Assertion : In a redox reaction, the oxidation number of the oxidant decreases, while that of reductant increases.
Reason : Oxidant gains electron (s) reductant loses electrons (s).
examples.solution: (a) Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — Directions — Q33
examples.problem:

examples.title: Assertion \& Reason — Directions — Q34
Passage Based Questions — Q35
examples.problem:
What is the oxidation number of P in $\mathrm{PO}_{4}^{3-}$ and S in $\mathrm{SO}_{4}^{2-}$ ?
(a) $\mathrm{P}=+5, \mathrm{~S}=+3$
(b) $\mathrm{P}=+3 . \mathrm{S}=+5$
(c) $\mathrm{P}=+5, \mathrm{~S}=+6$
(d) $\mathrm{P}=+6, \mathrm{~S}=+6$
examples.solution:
What is the oxidation number of P in $\mathrm{PO}_{4}^{3-}$ and S in $\mathrm{SO}_{4}^{2-}$ ?
(a) $\mathrm{P}=+5, \mathrm{~S}=+3$
(b) $\mathrm{P}=+3 . \mathrm{S}=+5$
(c) $\mathrm{P}=+5, \mathrm{~S}=+6$
(d) $\mathrm{P}=+6, \mathrm{~S}=+6$

examples.title: Passage Based Questions — Q36
examples.problem:
Oxygen has an oxidation state of +2 in the compound :
(a) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{F}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
examples.solution:
Oxygen has an oxidation state of +2 in the compound :
(a) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{F}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

examples.title: Passage Based Questions — Q37
examples.problem:
What is the correct increasing order of oxidation state of nitrogen in following compounds?
$\mathrm{NH}_{3}, \mathrm{~N}_{2}, \mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}, \mathrm{NO}_{2}$
(a) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{N}_{2}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}$
(b) $\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{NH}_{3}$
(c) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{N}_{2}$
(d) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{N}_{2}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}$
examples.solution:
What is the correct increasing order of oxidation state of nitrogen in following compounds?
$\mathrm{NH}_{3}, \mathrm{~N}_{2}, \mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}, \mathrm{NO}_{2}$
(a) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{N}_{2}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}$
(b) $\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{NH}_{3}$
(c) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{N}_{2}$
(d) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{N}_{2}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}$

examples.title: Passage Based Questions — Directions — Q38
examples.problem: Among $\mathrm{NH}_{3}, \mathrm{HNO}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{Mg}_{3} \mathrm{~N}_{2}$ how many molecules have nitrogen in negative oxidation state?
examples.solution:
2 Calculating the oxidation state of nitrogen in given molecules;
Oxidation state of N in $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ is
$x+3 \times(+1)=0$ or $x=-3$
Oxidation state on N in $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}$ is
$1+x+3 \times(-2)=0$ or $x=+5$
Oxidation state of N in $\mathrm{Mg}_{3} \mathrm{~N}_{2}$ is
$3 \times 2+2 x=0$ or $x=-3$
Thus 2 molecules (i.e. $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{Mg}_{3} \mathrm{~N}_{2}$ have nitrogen in negative oxidation state.

examples.title: Passage Based Questions — Directions — Q39
examples.problem: When $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}$ is converted into $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{CrO}_{4}$, then what is the change in oxidation number of chromium?
examples.solution:
0 O.N. of Cr in $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}: 2+2 x-14=0$
$2 x=12, x=6$
O.N. of Cr in $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{CrO}_{4}: 2+x-8=0$
$x=6$
∴ Change in O.N. of $\mathrm{Cr}=6-6=0$

examples.title: Passage Based Questions — Directions — Q40
examples.problem:
Consider the following reaction
$\mathrm{PbS}+n \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbSO}_{4}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
What is the value of coefficient ' $n$ ' in the above equation?
examples.solution:
4 Balanced equation is
$\mathrm{PbS}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbSO}_{4}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

examples.title: Passage Based Questions — Directions — Q41
examples.problem: Find the oxidation number of S in $\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3}\right)_{2} \mathrm{SO}$.
examples.solution:
0 Let the oxidation no. of S be $x$
O. N of $\mathrm{CH}_{3}=+1$
O. N of $\mathrm{O}=-2$
$2(+1)+x+(-2)=0$
$x=0$

examples.title: Passage Based Questions — Directions — Q42
examples.problem: Find the difference in oxidation states of nitrogen in nitrous acid and nitric acid.
examples.solution:
2 Nitrous acid $\mathrm{HNO}_{2}$
$(+1)+x+2(-2)=0$
$x=+3$
Nitric acid $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}$
$(+1)+x+3(-2)=0 \Rightarrow x=+5$
